1→//! Protocol types for the Shatter Rust frontend.
     2→//!
     3→//! These types match the JSON wire format defined in `shatter-core/src/protocol.rs`.
     4→//! The protocol uses newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON) over stdin/stdout between
     5→//! the core engine and this frontend.
     6→//!
     7→//! Like the Go frontend, we use flat structs with optional fields rather than
     8→//! tagged enums — simpler for a standalone frontend that only needs to parse
     9→//! requests and emit responses.
    10→
    11→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    12→use std::collections::HashMap;
    13→
    14→/// Well-known complex types beyond primitives and structural types.
    15→/// Matches `ComplexKind` in shatter-core/src/types.rs.
    16→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    17→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    18→pub enum ComplexKind {
    19→    Date, DateTime, Time, Duration,
    20→    RegExp, Char, Symbol,
    21→    BigInt, BigDecimal, Complex, Rational, Range,
    22→    Buffer, BitSet,
    23→    Error, Option, Result,
    24→    Closure, Iterator,
    25→    Url, IpAddress,
    26→    Uuid,
    27→    Path,
    28→    Money, SemVer, Email, MimeType, Color, GeoPoint, Locale,
    29→    Rune, GoByte,
    30→}
    31→
    32→/// Describes the type of a value, as reported by a language frontend.
    33→/// Matches `TypeInfo` in shatter-core/src/types.rs.
    34→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    35→#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    36→pub enum TypeInfo {
    37→    Int,
    38→    Float,
    39→    Str,
    40→    Bool,
    41→    Array { element: Box<TypeInfo> },
    42→    Object { fields: Vec<(String, TypeInfo)> },
    43→    Union { variants: Vec<TypeInfo> },
    44→    Nullable { inner: Box<TypeInfo> },
    45→    Complex {
    46→        #[serde(rename = "complex_kind")]
    47→        kind: ComplexKind,
    48→        #[serde(default)]
    49→        metadata: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
    50→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    51→        inner: Option<Box<TypeInfo>>,
    52→    },
    53→    Opaque {
    54→        label: String,
    55→    },
    56→    Unknown,
    57→}
    58→
    59→/// Binary operation kind for symbolic expressions.
    60→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    61→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    62→pub enum BinOpKind {
    63→    Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge,
    64→    Add, Sub, Mul, Div, Mod,
    65→    And, Or,
    66→    BitwiseAnd, BitwiseOr, BitwiseXor,
    67→    In, InstanceOf,
    68→}
    69→
    70→/// Unary operation kind for symbolic expressions.
    71→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    72→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    73→pub enum UnOpKind {
    74→    Not,
    75→    Neg,
    76→    BitwiseNot,
    77→    #[serde(rename = "typeof")]
    78→    TypeOf,
    79→}
    80→
    81→/// Constant value in a symbolic expression.
    82→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    83→#[serde(tag = "type", content = "value", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    84→pub enum ConstValue {
    85→    Int(i64),
    86→    Float(f64),
    87→    Str(String),
    88→    Bool(bool),
    89→    Null,
    90→}
    91→
    92→/// Symbolic expression tree for branch conditions.
    93→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    94→#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    95→pub enum SymExpr {
    96→    Param {
    97→        name: String,
    98→        #[serde(default)]
    99→        path: Vec<String>,
   100→    },
   101→    Const(ConstValue),
   102→    BinOp {
   103→        op: BinOpKind,
   104→        left: Box<SymExpr>,
   105→        right: Box<SymExpr>,
   106→    },
   107→    UnOp {
   108→        op: UnOpKind,
   109→        operand: Box<SymExpr>,
   110→    },
   111→    Call {
   112→        name: String,
   113→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   114→        receiver: Option<Box<SymExpr>>,
   115→        #[serde(default)]
   116→        args: Vec<SymExpr>,
   117→    },
   118→    Unknown,
   119→}
   120→
   121→/// Branch type in control flow.
   122→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   123→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   124→pub enum BranchType {
   125→    If,
   126→    ElseIf,
   127→    Switch,
   128→    Ternary,
   129→    LogicalAnd,
   130→    LogicalOr,
   131→    While,
   132→    For,
   133→}
   134→
   135→/// A branch point in a function's control flow.
   136→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   137→pub struct BranchInfo {
   138→    pub id: u32,
   139→    pub line: u32,
   140→    pub condition_text: String,
   141→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   142→    pub condition: Option<SymExpr>,
   143→    pub branch_type: BranchType,
   144→}
   145→
   146→/// Parameter info for a function.
   147→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   148→pub struct ParamInfo {
   149→    pub name: String,
   150→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
   151→    pub typ: TypeInfo,
   152→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   153→    pub type_name: Option<String>,
   154→}
   155→
   156→/// Kind of external dependency.
   157→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   158→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   159→pub enum DependencyKind {
   160→    FunctionCall,
   161→    MethodCall,
   162→    PropertyAccess,
   163→    ModuleImport,
   164→}
   165→
   166→/// An external dependency detected in a function body.
   167→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   168→pub struct ExternalDependency {
   169→    pub kind: DependencyKind,
   170→    pub symbol: String,
   171→    pub source_module: String,
   172→    pub return_type: TypeInfo,
   173→    pub param_types: Vec<TypeInfo>,
   174→    pub call_sites: Vec<u32>,
   175→}
   176→
   177→/// A literal constant extracted from source code for use as a candidate test input.
   178→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   179→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   180→pub enum LiteralValue {
   181→    Int { value: i64 },
   182→    Float { value: f64 },
   183→    Str { value: String },
   184→    Bool { value: bool },
   185→    Regex { pattern: String },
   186→}
   187→
   188→/// A detected cryptographic API boundary within a function.
   189→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   190→pub struct CryptoBoundary {
   191→    pub symbol: String,
   192→    pub source_module: String,
   193→    pub direction: String,
   194→    pub output: String,
   195→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "HashMap::is_empty")]
   196→    pub param_roles: HashMap<String, String>,
   197→    pub call_sites: Vec<u32>,
   198→}
   199→
   200→/// Analysis result for a single function.
   201→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   202→pub struct FunctionAnalysis {
   203→    pub name: String,
   204→    #[serde(default)]
   205→    pub exported: bool,
   206→    pub params: Vec<ParamInfo>,
   207→    pub branches: Vec<BranchInfo>,
   208→    pub dependencies: Vec<ExternalDependency>,
   209→    pub return_type: TypeInfo,
   210→    pub start_line: u32,
   211→    pub end_line: u32,
   212→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   213→    pub literals: Vec<LiteralValue>,
   214→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   215→    pub crypto_boundaries: Vec<CryptoBoundary>,
   216→}
   217→
   218→/// Current protocol version.
   219→pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: &str = "0.1.0";
   220→
   221→/// Frontend version.
   222→pub const FRONTEND_VERSION: &str = "0.1.0";
   223→
   224→/// Language identifier for this frontend.
   225→pub const FRONTEND_LANGUAGE: &str = "rust";
   226→
   227→/// A request message from the core engine to this frontend.
   228→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
   229→pub struct Request {
   230→    pub protocol_version: String,
   231→    pub id: u64,
   232→    pub command: String,
   233→
   234→    // Handshake fields
   235→    #[allow(dead_code)] // will be used when capability negotiation is implemented
   236→    #[serde(default)]
   237→    pub capabilities: Vec<String>,
   238→
   239→    // Analyze/Instrument/Execute fields
   240→    #[serde(default)]
   241→    pub file: Option<String>,
   242→    #[serde(default)]
   243→    pub function: Option<String>,
   244→
   245→    // Execute fields
   246→    #[serde(default)]
   247→    pub inputs: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   248→    #[serde(default)]
   249→    pub mocks: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   250→    /// Opaque context returned by a prior Setup command, if any.
   251→    #[allow(dead_code)] // will be used when setup+execute context passing is implemented
   252→    #[serde(default)]
   253→    pub setup_context: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   254→
   255→    // Setup fields
   256→    /// When to run setup relative to executions ("per_function" or "per_execution").
   257→    #[allow(dead_code)] // will be used when setup is implemented
   258→    #[serde(default)]
   259→    pub mode: Option<String>,
   260→
   261→    // Generate fields
   262→    /// Name of the type or parameter to generate a value for.
   263→    #[serde(default)]
   264→    pub name: Option<String>,
   265→    /// Whether the generator targets a type name or a parameter name.
   266→    #[allow(dead_code)] // parsed from protocol but not used in dispatch logic yet
   267→    #[serde(default)]
   268→    pub kind: Option<String>,
   269→    /// Opaque recipe state from a prior generate call, enabling stateful generators.
   270→    #[serde(default)]
   271→    pub recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   272→
   273→    // Project context
   274→    /// Detected project root directory, if any.
   275→    #[allow(dead_code)] // will be used when full analysis is implemented
   276→    #[serde(default)]
   277→    pub project_root: Option<String>,
   278→}
   279→
   280→/// A response message from this frontend to the core engine.
   281→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   282→pub struct Response {
   283→    pub protocol_version: String,
   284→    pub id: u64,
   285→    pub status: String,
   286→
   287→    // Handshake fields
   288→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   289→    pub frontend_version: Option<String>,
   290→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   291→    pub language: Option<String>,
   292→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   293→    pub capabilities: Option<Vec<String>>,
   294→
   295→    // Setup fields
   296→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   297→    pub setup_context: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   298→
   299→    // Generate fields
   300→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   301→    pub value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   302→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   303→    pub generator_id: Option<String>,
   304→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   305→    pub recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   306→
   307→    // Instrument fields
   308→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   309→    pub instrumented: Option<bool>,
   310→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   311→    pub output_file: Option<String>,
   312→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   313→    pub instrumentable_line_count: Option<u32>,
   314→
   315→    // Analyze fields
   316→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   317→    pub functions: Option<Vec<FunctionAnalysis>>,
   318→
   319→    // Execute result fields — flattened to match the core's wire format.
   320→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   321→    pub return_value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   322→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   323→    pub thrown_error: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   324→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   325→    pub branch_path: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   326→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   327→    pub lines_executed: Option<Vec<u32>>,
   328→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   329→    pub calls_to_external: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   330→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   331→    pub path_constraints: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   332→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   333→    pub side_effects: Option<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   334→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   335→    pub performance: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   336→
   337→    // Error fields
   338→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   339→    pub code: Option<String>,
   340→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   341→    pub message: Option<String>,
   342→}
   343→
   344→impl Response {
   345→    /// Create a base response with protocol version and request ID.
   346→    pub fn base(id: u64) -> Self {
   347→        Self {
   348→            protocol_version: PROTOCOL_VERSION.to_string(),
   349→            id,
   350→            status: String::new(),
   351→            frontend_version: None,
   352→            language: None,
   353→            capabilities: None,
   354→            setup_context: None,
   355→            value: None,
   356→            generator_id: None,
   357→            recipe: None,
   358→            instrumented: None,
   359→            output_file: None,
   360→            instrumentable_line_count: None,
   361→            functions: None,
   362→            return_value: None,
   363→            thrown_error: None,
   364→            branch_path: None,
   365→            lines_executed: None,
   366→            calls_to_external: None,
   367→            path_constraints: None,
   368→            side_effects: None,
   369→            performance: None,
   370→            code: None,
   371→            message: None,
   372→        }
   373→    }
   374→}
   375→
   376→#[cfg(test)]
   377→mod tests {
   378→    use super::*;
   379→
   380→    fn round_trip<T: Serialize + for<'de> Deserialize<'de> + PartialEq + std::fmt::Debug>(
   381→        value: &T,
   382→    ) {
   383→        let json = serde_json::to_string(value).expect("serialize");
   384→        let deserialized: T = serde_json::from_str(&json).expect("deserialize");
   385→        assert_eq!(*value, deserialized, "round-trip failed for json: {json}");
   386→    }
   387→
   388→    #[test]
   389→    fn typeinfo_opaque_round_trips() {
   390→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Opaque {
   391→            label: "net.Socket".to_string(),
   392→        });
   393→    }
   394→
   395→    #[test]
   396→    fn typeinfo_opaque_serializes_with_correct_kind() {
   397→        let ti = TypeInfo::Opaque {
   398→            label: "fs.FileHandle".to_string(),
   399→        };
   400→        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::to_value(&ti).expect("serialize");
   401→        assert_eq!(json["kind"], "opaque");
   402→        assert_eq!(json["label"], "fs.FileHandle");
   403→    }
   404→
   405→    #[test]
   406→    fn typeinfo_opaque_inside_array_round_trips() {
   407→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Array {
   408→            element: Box::new(TypeInfo::Opaque {
   409→                label: "stream.Readable".to_string(),
   410→            }),
   411→        });
   412→    }
   413→
   414→    #[test]
   415→    fn typeinfo_opaque_inside_nullable_round_trips() {
   416→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Nullable {
   417→            inner: Box::new(TypeInfo::Opaque {
   418→                label: "channel".to_string(),
   419→            }),
   420→        });
   421→    }
   422→
   423→    #[test]
   424→    fn typeinfo_opaque_inside_object_round_trips() {
   425→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Object {
   426→            fields: vec![
   427→                (
   428→                    "conn".into(),
   429→                    TypeInfo::Opaque {
   430→                        label: "pg.Client".to_string(),
   431→                    },
   432→                ),
   433→                ("name".into(), TypeInfo::Str),
   434→            ],
   435→        });
   436→    }
   437→
   438→    #[test]
   439→    fn existing_typeinfo_variants_still_round_trip() {
   440→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Int);
   441→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Float);
   442→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Str);
   443→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Bool);
   444→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Unknown);
   445→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Array {
   446→            element: Box::new(TypeInfo::Int),
   447→        });
   448→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Nullable {
   449→            inner: Box::new(TypeInfo::Str),
   450→        });
   451→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Object {
   452→            fields: vec![("x".into(), TypeInfo::Int)],
   453→        });
   454→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Union {
   455→            variants: vec![TypeInfo::Str, TypeInfo::Int],
   456→        });
   457→    }
   458→
   459→    #[test]
   460→    fn typeinfo_complex_still_round_trips() {
   461→        round_trip(&TypeInfo::Complex {
   462→            kind: ComplexKind::Date,
   463→            metadata: HashMap::new(),
   464→            inner: None,
   465→        });
   466→    }
   467→
   468→    #[test]
   469→    fn opaque_in_function_analysis_json_deserializes() {
   470→        // Verify TypeInfo::Opaque works when embedded in a FunctionAnalysis-shaped JSON,
   471→        // parsed as a generic Value and then extracting the type field.
   472→        let json = r#"{"kind": "opaque", "label": "stream.Readable"}"#;
   473→        let param_type: TypeInfo = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize param type");
   474→        assert_eq!(
   475→            param_type,
   476→            TypeInfo::Opaque {
   477→                label: "stream.Readable".to_string(),
   478→            }
   479→        );
   480→
   481→        // Nested inside an object field (simulating a return_type in analysis results)
   482→        let nested_json = r#"{"kind": "object", "fields": [["conn", {"kind": "opaque", "label": "pg.Client"}], ["ready", {"kind": "bool"}]]}"#;
   483→        let nested: TypeInfo = serde_json::from_str(nested_json).expect("deserialize nested");
   484→        if let TypeInfo::Object { fields } = &nested {
   485→            assert_eq!(fields.len(), 2);
   486→            assert_eq!(
   487→                fields[0].1,
   488→                TypeInfo::Opaque {
   489→                    label: "pg.Client".to_string(),
   490→                }
   491→            );
   492→        } else {
   493→            panic!("expected Object, got {:?}", nested);
   494→        }
   495→    }
   496→
   497→    // -- Request deserialization tests for new commands --
   498→
   499→    #[test]
   500→    fn setup_request_deserializes() {
   501→        let json = r#"{"protocol_version":"0.1.0","id":20,"command":"setup","file":"./setup.ts","function":"processOrder","mode":"per_function"}"#;
   502→        let req: Request = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize setup");
   503→        assert_eq!(req.id, 20);
   504→        assert_eq!(req.command, "setup");
   505→        assert_eq!(req.file.as_deref(), Some("./setup.ts"));
   506→        assert_eq!(req.function.as_deref(), Some("processOrder"));
   507→        assert_eq!(req.mode.as_deref(), Some("per_function"));
   508→    }
   509→
   510→    #[test]
   511→    fn teardown_request_deserializes() {
   512→        let json = r#"{"protocol_version":"0.1.0","id":22,"command":"teardown","function":"processOrder"}"#;
   513→        let req: Request = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize teardown");
   514→        assert_eq!(req.id, 22);
   515→        assert_eq!(req.command, "teardown");
   516→        assert_eq!(req.function.as_deref(), Some("processOrder"));
   517→    }
   518→
   519→    #[test]
   520→    fn generate_request_type_name_deserializes() {
   521→        let json = r#"{"protocol_version":"0.1.0","id":23,"command":"generate","file":"./gen.ts","name":"User","kind":"type_name"}"#;
   522→        let req: Request = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize generate type_name");
   523→        assert_eq!(req.id, 23);
   524→        assert_eq!(req.command, "generate");
   525→        assert_eq!(req.file.as_deref(), Some("./gen.ts"));
   526→        assert_eq!(req.name.as_deref(), Some("User"));
   527→        assert_eq!(req.kind.as_deref(), Some("type_name"));
   528→    }
   529→
   530→    #[test]
   531→    fn execute_request_with_setup_context_deserializes() {
   532→        let json = r#"{"protocol_version":"0.1.0","id":25,"command":"execute","function":"fn1","inputs":[1],"mocks":[],"setup_context":{"db":"conn_42"}}"#;
   533→        let req: Request = serde_json::from_str(json).expect("deserialize execute with setup_context");
   534→        assert_eq!(req.setup_context, Some(serde_json::json!({"db": "conn_42"})));
   535→    }
   536→

    53→    fn from(mode: crate::config::SetupMode) -> Self {
    54→        match mode {
    55→            crate::config::SetupMode::PerFunction => SetupLevel::Function,
    56→            crate::config::SetupMode::PerExecution => SetupLevel::Execution,
    57→        }
    58→    }
    59→}
    60→
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→// Request: Core → Frontend
    63→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→
    65→/// A request message sent from the core engine to a language frontend.
    66→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    67→pub struct Request {
    68→    /// Protocol version for compatibility checking.
    69→    pub protocol_version: String,
    70→    /// Unique identifier for correlating responses to requests.
    71→    pub id: u64,
    72→    /// The command to execute.
    73→    #[serde(flatten)]
    74→    pub command: Command,
    75→}
    76→
    77→/// Commands the core can send to a frontend.
    78→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    79→#[serde(tag = "command", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    80→pub enum Command {
    81→    /// Initial handshake to negotiate protocol version and capabilities.
    82→    Handshake {
    83→        /// Capabilities supported by the core.
    84→        capabilities: Vec<String>,
    85→    },
    86→    /// Analyze a function to extract type information, branches, and dependencies.
    87→    Analyze {
    88→        /// Path to the source file.
    89→        file: String,
    90→        /// Name of the function to analyze. If absent, analyze all exported functions.
    91→        function: Option<String>,
    92→        /// Detected project root directory, if any.
    93→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    94→        project_root: Option<String>,
    95→    },
    96→    /// Instrument a function for symbolic constraint tracking.
    97→    Instrument {
    98→        /// Path to the source file.
    99→        file: String,
   100→        /// Name of the function to instrument.
   101→        function: String,
   102→        /// Mock configurations for external dependencies.
   103→        mocks: Vec<MockConfig>,
   104→        /// Detected project root directory, if any.
   105→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   106→        project_root: Option<String>,
   107→    },
   108→    /// Execute an instrumented function with specific inputs and mocks.
   109→    Execute {
   110→        /// Fully qualified function identifier.
   111→        function: String,
   112→        /// Input values for the function parameters (JSON-encoded).
   113→        inputs: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   114→        /// Mock configurations for external dependencies.
   115→        mocks: Vec<MockConfig>,
   116→        /// Stack of active setup contexts from enclosing Setup commands, if any.
   117→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   118→        setup_context: Option<SetupContextStack>,
   119→    },
   120→    /// Run a setup file to initialize state before function execution.
   121→    Setup {
   122→        /// Path to the setup file.
   123→        file: String,
   124→        /// Scope identifier (function name, file path, or session label).
   125→        scope: String,
   126→        /// Lifecycle level for this setup.
   127→        level: SetupLevel,
   128→        /// Detected project root directory, if any.
   129→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   130→        project_root: Option<String>,
   131→        /// Parent context stack from enclosing setup levels, if any.
   132→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   133→        parent_context: Option<SetupContextStack>,
   134→    },
   135→    /// Tear down state established by a prior Setup command.
   136→    Teardown {
   137→        /// Scope identifier matching the corresponding Setup command.
   138→        scope: String,
   139→        /// Lifecycle level matching the corresponding Setup command.
   140→        level: SetupLevel,
   141→    },
   142→    /// Invoke a custom generator to produce a value for a type or parameter.
   143→    Generate {
   144→        /// Path to the generator file.
   145→        file: String,
   146→        /// Name of the type or parameter to generate a value for.
   147→        name: String,
   148→        /// Whether this generator targets a type name or a parameter name.
   149→        kind: GeneratorKind,
   150→        /// Reconstruction recipe from a previous generation.
   151→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   152→        recipe: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   153→        /// Detected project root directory, if any.
   154→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   155→        project_root: Option<String>,
   156→    },
   157→    /// Request graceful shutdown of the frontend process.
   158→    Shutdown,
   159→}
   160→
   161→/// Whether a generator targets a type name or a parameter name.
   162→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   163→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   164→pub enum GeneratorKind {
   165→    /// Generator for a named type (e.g., "User").
   166→    TypeName,
   167→    /// Generator for a named parameter (e.g., "authToken").
   168→    ParamName,
   169→}
   170→
   171→/// Configuration for mocking an external dependency during execution.
   172→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   173→pub struct MockConfig {
   174→    /// Fully qualified symbol name of the dependency to mock.
   175→    pub symbol: String,
   176→    /// Pre-configured return values to cycle through.
   177→    pub return_values: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   178→    /// Whether to record arguments passed to the mock.
   179→    pub should_track_calls: bool,
   180→    /// Default behavior when return_values are exhausted.
   181→    pub default_behavior: MockBehavior,
   182→}
   183→
   184→/// How a mock should behave when its pre-configured return values are exhausted.
   185→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   186→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   187→pub enum MockBehavior {
   188→    /// Return a generated value based on the return type.
   189→    ReturnGenerated,
   190→    /// Repeat the last return value.
   191→    RepeatLast,
   192→    /// Throw an error.
   193→    ThrowError,
   194→    /// Call through to the real implementation.
   195→    Passthrough,
   196→}
   197→
   198→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   199→// Response: Frontend → Core
   200→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   - Change `mode: Option<String>` to `level: Option<SetupLevel>`
